learning_outcomes.description: Take account of current debates in palaeoecology through understanding and reflection on the content of lectures, practicals and required readings.
Develop skills of research, analysis and argument in the areas of palaeoecology.
Describe key concepts in fire ecology, through understanding and reflection on the content of lectures, practicals and required readings.
Develop skills of research, analysis and argument in the areas of fire ecology.
Describe how key concepts in palaeoecology and fire ecology are crucial for effective natural resource management and conservation.
Describe how fire can impact Australian landscapes and key issues that need to be taken into account for planned burns.

assessments.due_date:
1) Practical Workbook 1 31/07/2026 4:00 pm

2) Practical Workbook 2 18/09/2026 4:00 pm

3) Practical Workbook 3 9/10/2026 4:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
There will be three practical assessments based on interactive online exercises that demonstrate core concepts of this course. This will include participation in a Q&A session which will occur during the scheduled practical session.

Students will complete their practical workbook and must submit their completed workbook on the Friday of the week that the practical is delivered.

Each practical workbook is weighted equally.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission via Blackbaoad only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission details. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Using the information and data provided in class, students will develop a report outlining a fire management plan that will address key conservation and hazard minimisation goals that are relevant to SE Queensland. The report will be based on a desktop study of Regional Ecosystems in an area of SE Queensland.

The report will be graded based on a criteria posted on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission via Inspera only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission details. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

End of Semester Exam Period

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre.
assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a final exam that will cover key aspects of the course based on the lecture material.

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre. You must be able to attend one of these venues. Please refer to the Off-Campus exams information page for further information. Please note a digital exam is not available for this assessment.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
